entities.name: Lilith

attributes.role: female demoness; associated with mirror ritual

entities.raw_quote: Hoc est primum speculum Lilit et suorum virorum suorumque militum seniorum.

entities.name: Lylet

attributes.role: demon conjured via mirror; claims connection to Nocma and Arieth

entities.raw_quote: Coniuro te, Lylet, ac socios tuos... vt in quacumque hora te vocauero, in hoc speculo michi appareas cum tuis sociis...

entities.name: Nocma

attributes.role: mother of Lylet; referenced in conjuration

entities.raw_quote: ...per illud sacramentum quod tu Nocma matri tue fecisti verbo...

entities.name: Arieth

attributes.role: father of Lylet; invoked in conjuration

entities.raw_quote: ...per honorem patris tui Arieth...

entities.name: speculum calibeum limpidum et politum

attributes.material: polished metal mirror; made on Thursday or Friday of crescent moon

entities.raw_quote: ...habeas tecum speculum calibeum limpidum et politum, factum luna 4a uel 7a vel xiija.a...

entities.name: cristallus

attributes.preparation: washed with wine; inscribed with divine names in olive oil

entities.raw_quote: Si vis scire de omni re de qua dubitaueris, accipe puerum virginem et cristallum, quem laua cum vino.

entities.name: cultellus de Venere factus
entities.raw_quote: ...abscide cor cum cultello qui sit de Venere facto...

entities.name: Onel, On, Adonay, Emanuel

attributes.usage: inscribed on ritual tools; part of protective/invocatory formulae

entities.raw_quote: Onel, On, Adonay, Emanuel, sanctus deus fortis...

entities.name: Basyon

attributes.role: divine name invoked in binding pueri

entities.raw_quote: Coniuro te, virgo iuuenis, per deum verum Basyon...

entities.name: Ioth, Nabnoth

attributes.role: binding names used to constrain demons; referenced as Salomon’s vessel-binding formulae

entities.raw_quote: ...per hec duo nomina: Ioth [et] Nabnoth, per que Salomon constringebat in vase vitreo demones.

entities.name: Tetragramaton

attributes.role: central divine name; appears repeatedly across rituals

entities.raw_quote: ...per hoc nomen domini: Tetragramaton...

entities.name: Ely, Eloy, Eloe

attributes.role: divine names invoked in binding and consecration rites

entities.raw_quote: ...per hec sacratissima nomina: Ely, Eloy, Messyas, Sother, Emanuel, Sabaoth, Adonay...

entities.name: EmanuEl
entities.raw_quote: ...Christus, Deus fortis, Emanuel...

entities.name: Alpha et O

attributes.role: apocalyptic divine title; used in nearly all conjurations

entities.raw_quote: Tu qui es deus, Alpha et O, fac hoc speculum crescere et clarescere...

entities.name: Balbieit, Zelans, Zelles, Dimedero, Cadar

attributes.role: demonic names invoked in Lilith mirror ritual

entities.raw_quote: ...Christus  vincit ipsum  Balbieit  Zelans, Zelles, Dimedero, Cadar...

entities.name: Dedeon

attributes.role: invoked in crystal scrying; possibly angelic

entities.raw_quote: Coniuro vos in nomine Dedeon, et dij Egarias et Semisonay...

entities.name: Bessabes

attributes.role: invoked to bind angels to crystal

entities.raw_quote: Coniuro vos in nomine Bessabes, et Hint, et Serem...

entities.name: Egippia, Benoham, Beanke

attributes.role: list of 25 names inscribed on pueri’s fingernail in No. 27-A

entities.raw_quote: ...Egippia, Benoham, Beanke (vel Beanre), Reranressym...

entities.name: Discobermath

attributes.role: demonic name invoked in final binding incantation

entities.raw_quote: ...aperite nobis, Discobermath, Archidemath, Fritath...

entities.name: Neyilon

attributes.role: demon invoked in Pluto’s Key ritual

entities.raw_quote: O spiritus Neyilon [et] Achalas, accipite sacrificium...

entities.name: Pluto

attributes.role: infernal ruler; key named after him

entities.raw_quote: No. 26. Key of Pluto, to open all locks...

entities.name: serraginem
entities.raw_quote: ...accipietc magister serraginem bene mundatam intus et extra...

entities.name: corrigia ovina

attributes.usage: bound around pueri’s finger with written names

entities.raw_quote: ...liga corrigiam ouinam, quam ad tres missas quas perfecte audiuisti tecum habuisti...

entities.name: cattus niger
entities.raw_quote: Studeas invenire vnam cattam nigram, natam in mense Martij...

entities.name: elyotropia

attributes.usage: herb used to fill eye sockets of black cat for invisibility

entities.raw_quote: ...et impleb foramina oculorum semine cuiusdam herbe que elyotropia dicitur...

entities.name: sanguis humanus

attributes.usage: mixed with water to irrigate white herb in invisibility ritual

entities.raw_quote: ...rigad cum sangwine humano et aqua commistis...

entities.name: cera virginea
entities.raw_quote: ...quem in cera reconde ex una parte virginea.

entities.name: oleum olivae

attributes.usage: used to inscribe divine names on crystal or mirror

entities.raw_quote: ...scribe in illo cum oleo oliue hec nomina...

entities.name: fenestrata circulus

attributes.description: ritual circle drawn on ground for scrying rituals

entities.raw_quote: ...facias circulum in terra...

entities.name: figurae Salomonis

attributes.description: pentagrams or hexagrams used for protection and binding

entities.raw_quote: ...circumdetur... sigillum Salomonis...

entities.name: quadriuium

attributes.role: ritual site for burying tools/spirits; crossroads

entities.raw_quote: ...defer ad quadriuium vel ad sepulc[r]um...

entities.name: sepulcrum hominis interfecti

attributes.role: ritual site for Lilith mirror; burial place of murdered man

entities.raw_quote: ...vel ad sepulc[r]um alicuius hominis interfecti...

entities.name: puer puellaque virgines

attributes.role: child seers used in scrying; must be chaste and legitimate-born

entities.raw_quote: Habeas puerum uel puellam virginem...

entities.name: magister

attributes.role: ritualist; conjures spirits, interrogates pueri

entities.raw_quote: Magister inspiciat in speculo et videbit militem armatum...

entities.name: Clm 849

attributes.role: Munich codex; source of the rituals

entities.page_ref: implied by Kieckhefer’s reference to CLM 849 in title
entities.raw_quote: [No direct quote, but context confirms this is from Munich, Bayerische Staatsbibliothek, CLM 849]

summary: This chunk contains detailed excerpts from the Munich Handbook (CLM 849), focusing on spirit conjuration via scrying tools—especially mirrors and crystals—and child mediums. It includes rituals for invisibility, theft detection, and infernal communication, with prominent invocation of divine names (e.g., Tetragramaton, Alpha et O) and demonic hierarchies (Lilith/Lylet). Rituals require specific timing (crescent moon, Friday), ritual purity, and protective circles. Connections to Salomonic tradition are evident in sigils and binding formulas.
key_passages: Coniuro te, Lylet, ac socios tuos, per Alpha et O, per primum et nouissimum... vt in quacumque hora te vocauero, in hoc speculo michi appareas cum tuis sociis...
Magister inspiciat in speculo et videbit militem armatum in equo sedentem et armigerum suum commitantem secum.
Si vis scire de omni re de qua dubitaueris, accipe puerum virginem et cristallum, quem laua cum vino.
Coniuro te, virgo iuuenis, per deum verum Basyon, et per nomen On, Berion, Sabaoth, Adonay...
Hoc est primum speculum Lilit et suorum virorum suorumque militum seniorum.
